description: Well-modelled Dart value types (domain primitives) for entities usually left as raw String/int — email, IBAN, and other ISO/RFC/GS1-standardised identifiers. Parse, don't validate: every instance is guaranteed well-formed.
rules: Parse, don't validate: construct every type through its `tryParse` (returns null on invalid) or `parse` (throws a typed FormatException) factory. There is no public constructor, so a value that exists is guaranteed well-formed — the same guarantee `Uri` gives for URLs.

Single-primitive types (Email, Iban, ...) are zero-cost extension types over String; multi-part types (coordinates, ...) are immutable classes. Both expose value equality, hashCode, and a canonical string form.
Types normalise on parse, so equality is canonical: `Iban.parse('gb82 west …') == Iban.parse('GB82WEST…')`. Compare parsed values, never the raw strings you parsed them from.
Check-digit types validate the real standard, not a regex shape: IBAN mod-97, card numbers Luhn, ISBN/EAN/ISSN check digits. A value that parses has passed its checksum.
Pure Dart, minimal deps — runs in CLI, server, web, and Flutter. Opinionated integrations (JSON, fpdart, Hive, Flutter form validators) are opt-in and never forced on core consumers.
